O Beauty Co - Fragrance

Range overviewSoft scentsWarm scentsOud scentsBold scentsSelling tipsMood activity
Module 1

O Beauty Co - Fragrance

Learn the range, match the mood, and sell it simply.

Lesson 1

Range overview

O Beauty Co Fragrance is a luxury fragrance range designed for store selling.

The range has 10 fragrances. All 10 are available in 50ml. Five fragrances are also available in 100ml.

The simplest way to sell the range is to start with mood: soft, warm, elegant, or bold.

Sizes and price

  • 50ml: all 10 fragrances, R599
  • 100ml: Vanilla Amber, Star Dust, Royal Oud, Milky Cloud, and Carnal Rose, R999
  • Use 50ml as the easier starting point when the customer is still deciding

100ml scents

  • Vanilla Amber
  • Star Dust
  • Royal Oud
  • Milky Cloud
  • Carnal Rose

50ml-only scents

  • Havana Nights
  • Satin Oud
  • Raw Suede
  • Night Smoke
  • Moon Leather

Scent words

  • Soft and airy: clean, light, easy to wear
  • Warm and romantic: smooth, cosy, floral warmth
  • Elegant oud: rich, woody, dressed up
  • Bold and smoky: dark, leather, statement scent

Opening questions

  • Do you want something soft and clean, warm and cosy, elegant and woody, or bold and smoky
  • Is this for every day, a gift, or evening wear
  • Do you want a smaller starting size or the larger size

Starter example

Customer: I want a fragrance gift, but I do not know where to start.

Best response: Start with a mood question, narrow the family, and show only two or three options first.

Lesson 2

Soft and airy scents

This is the easiest place to start when the customer wants something clean, light, or easy to wear.

Milky Cloud feels soft and creamy. Star Dust feels clean and polished.

Milky Cloud

The Drift

Soft as breath, ephemeral as a sigh. Coconut milk, white musk, and airy heliotrope conjure the haze between pleasure and peace. A lullaby for the senses.

How it smells

Soft, creamy, and calm.

Key notes

Coconut milk, white musk, and heliotrope.

Size

50ml and 100ml.

Star Dust

The Wonder

Glittering silence. Soft aldehydes, crystalline florals, and a shimmer of powder wrap you in celestial magic. A perfume that feels like catching stars on your skin.

How it smells

Clean, light, and slightly powdery.

Key notes

Florals, powder, and aldehydes.

Size

50ml and 100ml.

Customer cues

  • I want something clean
  • I want a fragrance for every day
  • I do not want anything too heavy
  • I like soft scents more than dark scents

How to talk about it

  • Start with Star Dust if the customer wants clean and polished
  • Start with Milky Cloud if the customer wants soft and cosy
  • Use words like light, calm, fresh, and easy to wear

When to recommend

Recommend this family when the customer wants an everyday scent that feels soft, clean, or light.

Lesson 3

Warm and romantic scents

This part of the range covers the warmer, smoother, more romantic fragrances.

Carnal Rose brings floral warmth with spice. Vanilla Amber brings comfort and sweetness.

Carnal Rose

The Heartbeat

A rose in full bloom, dripping with desire. Laced with saffron, pepper, and crimson petals, it captures the moment when lust and love become indistinguishable.

How it smells

Floral, warm, and slightly spicy.

Key notes

Rose petals, saffron, and pepper.

Size

50ml and 100ml.

Vanilla Amber

The First Glow

The warmth of a candlelit embrace. Sweet vanilla curls around molten amber, glowing like skin kissed by fading daylight. It is comfort, wrapped in temptation.

How it smells

Warm, sweet, and smooth.

Key notes

Vanilla and amber.

Size

50ml and 100ml.

Customer cues

  • I want something warm
  • I like floral scents but want more depth
  • I want something smooth and comforting
  • I do not want anything smoky

How to talk about it

  • Use Carnal Rose when the customer wants floral with more character
  • Use Vanilla Amber when the customer wants warm comfort without smoke
  • Keep the comparison simple: rose and spice versus vanilla and amber

When to recommend

Recommend this family when the customer wants warmth, romance, or comfort without moving into darker scent profiles.

Lesson 4

Elegant oud scents

These fragrances cover the oud part of the range.

Royal Oud is deeper and more commanding. Satin Oud is softer and smoother.

Royal Oud

The Power Play

Commanding and composed. A deeper, darker oud infused with incense, leather, and aged resins. Regal, rooted, and undeniably magnetic.

How it smells

Deep, woody, and more intense.

Key notes

Incense, leather, and resins.

Size

50ml and 100ml.

Satin Oud

The Caress

A veil of sensual elegance. Oud is draped in creamy sandalwood and delicate florals, luxurious, intimate, and endlessly smooth. It whispers, never shouts.

How it smells

Smooth, creamy, and elegant.

Key notes

Sandalwood and florals.

Size

50ml only.

Customer cues

  • I want something rich and elegant
  • I like woody scents
  • I want oud, but not something too sharp
  • I want a dressed-up fragrance

How to talk about it

  • Use Satin Oud when the customer wants a softer introduction to oud
  • Use Royal Oud when the customer wants more depth and presence
  • Keep the comparison simple: smoother oud versus stronger oud

When to recommend

Recommend this family when the customer wants a rich, woody fragrance with a more elegant feel.

Lesson 5

Bold and smoky scents

This lesson covers the darkest, smokiest, and most statement-driven part of the range.

Use this family when the customer wants leather, smoke, or a stronger evening scent.

Havana Nights

The Finale

A memory steeped in rum, tobacco, and dancing heat. Patchouli and golden citrus swirl in the smoky air of a warm Caribbean night, where stories are born and never told.

How it smells

Warm, smoky, and lively.

Key notes

Rum, tobacco, patchouli, and citrus.

Size

50ml only.

Raw Suede

The Seduction Begins

Supple, untamed, unforgettable. Earthy suede meets warm spice, evoking bare skin and whispered promises. Rough around the edges, dangerously smooth at the core.

How it smells

Warm, spicy, and sensual.

Key notes

Suede and spice.

Size

50ml only.

Moon Leather

The Midnight Ride

Cool and compelling. Smoky leather meets lunar herbs and cold metal, like a motorcycle slicing through silver moonlight. It is freedom in motion.

How it smells

Cool, smoky, and edgy.

Key notes

Smoky leather and herbs.

Size

50ml only.

Night Smoke

The Spell

Mystery rising. Birch tar, vetiver, and black tea swirl like incense in the dark. It is hypnotic, haunting, and addictive.

How it smells

Dark, smoky, and mysterious.

Key notes

Vetiver, birch tar, and black tea.

Size

50ml only.

Customer cues

  • I want a statement scent
  • I like leather or smoke
  • I want something for evening
  • I do not want anything soft or airy

How to talk about it

  • Ask if the customer wants warm smoke or cool leather
  • Use Havana Nights and Raw Suede for warmer recommendations
  • Use Moon Leather and Night Smoke for cooler, darker recommendations

When to recommend

Recommend this family when the customer wants something bold, smoky, leather-led, or more evening-focused.

Lesson 6

Sell it simply

The goal is simple: ask clear questions, use simple scent words, and guide the customer without overloading them.

When the language stays clear, the recommendation feels easier and more confident for both you and the customer.

Simple sales flow

  • Ask what mood they want: soft, warm, elegant, or bold
  • Ask when they want to wear it: every day, gift, or evening
  • Start with two or three options, not the full range
  • End with one clear recommendation and one backup option

Usage tips to share

  • Spray on pulse points like the neck or wrists
  • Let the scent settle before deciding
  • Compare only a small number of scents at one time
  • Translate technical note words straight away

Soft and airy

  • This feels clean, light, and easy to wear
  • This is a good place to start if you do not want anything heavy

Warm and romantic

  • This feels warmer and smoother on the skin
  • This is a good choice if you want comfort or a softer evening scent

Elegant oud

  • This feels richer and more dressed up
  • This is a good oud option if you want something smooth or deeper

Bold and smoky

  • This is darker, smokier, and more statement-led
  • This is a better choice if you want leather, smoke, or evening energy

When to lead with 50ml

  • When the customer is still deciding
  • When they want a smaller gift option
  • When they want a first try before going bigger

When to lead with 100ml

  • When the customer already loves the scent
  • When they want a larger fragrance for regular use
  • When they want one of the five fragrances in the larger size

Gift shopper

Customer: I need a gift, but I do not know what kind of scent they like.

Best response: Start with a mood question, then lead with a 50ml option if the customer is still unsure.

Everyday shopper

Customer: I want something clean and easy to wear every day.

Best response: Start with Star Dust, then show Milky Cloud if they want something softer and more comforting.

Evening shopper

Customer: I want something richer for evening.

Best response: Choose between warm, elegant, or bold first, then compare one clear option against one backup.

Final activity

Mood matcher

Choose a mood and a moment. The matcher gives you a lead scent and a backup option so you can practise ending with one clear recommendation.

Step 1: pick a mood

Step 2: pick the moment

Current match

Choose a mood + choose a moment

Use this final activity to practise landing on one lead scent and one backup option with confidence.

Make a match

Choose one mood and one moment to reveal the best fragrance to lead with, plus a backup option.

Soft and airy match

Star Dust

It feels clean, light, and easy to wear every day without becoming too heavy.

How to sell it

Lead with Star Dust first, then offer Milky Cloud if the customer wants something softer and more comforting.

Backup option

Milky Cloud

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Soft and airy match

Milky Cloud

It feels gentle, soft, and safe when the shopper wants a gift without too much intensity.

How to sell it

Lead with Milky Cloud as the softer gift option, then show Star Dust if they want something cleaner and more polished.

Backup option

Star Dust

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Soft and airy

Soft and airy match

Milky Cloud

It keeps the mood soft for evening rather than moving into smoke, leather, or heavier warmth.

How to sell it

Use Milky Cloud when they want an evening fragrance that still feels calm and easy, not dramatic.

Backup option

Star Dust

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Soft and airy

Warm and romantic match

Vanilla Amber

It gives smooth warmth that feels comforting enough for regular wear.

How to sell it

Start with Vanilla Amber for the easiest everyday warmth, then show Carnal Rose if they want a warmer floral twist.

Backup option

Carnal Rose

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Warm and romantic

Warm and romantic match

Vanilla Amber

It is warm and crowd-friendly, which makes it easier to gift when the shopper wants something comforting.

How to sell it

Use Vanilla Amber as the safer warm gift option, with Carnal Rose as the more floral alternative.

Backup option

Carnal Rose

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Warm and romantic

Warm and romantic match

Carnal Rose

It brings more character and presence for evening while still staying warm rather than smoky.

How to sell it

Lead with Carnal Rose for evening warmth, then keep Vanilla Amber as the smoother backup choice.

Backup option

Vanilla Amber

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Warm and romantic

Elegant oud match

Satin Oud

It is the smoother, easier entry into oud for someone who still wants something wearable.

How to sell it

Use Satin Oud as the everyday oud, then offer Royal Oud only if they want more depth and intensity.

Backup option

Royal Oud

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Elegant oud

Elegant oud match

Satin Oud

It feels rich and elegant, but it is still the gentler oud recommendation for gifting.

How to sell it

Start with Satin Oud for the easier oud gift recommendation, and keep Royal Oud for shoppers asking for something stronger.

Backup option

Royal Oud

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Elegant oud

Elegant oud match

Royal Oud

It feels darker, more dressed up, and more intense for evening wear.

How to sell it

Lead with Royal Oud for evening depth, then show Satin Oud if they want the same family in a smoother style.

Backup option

Satin Oud

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Elegant oud

Bold and smoky match

Raw Suede

It keeps a bold mood, but it is easier to wear every day than the darkest smoky options.

How to sell it

Use Raw Suede first for a warmer bold everyday choice, then offer Havana Nights if they want more liveliness.

Backup option

Havana Nights

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Bold and smoky

Bold and smoky match

Havana Nights

It feels warm, lively, and memorable, which works well when the shopper wants a bold gift option.

How to sell it

Lead with Havana Nights as the more expressive gift option, and use Raw Suede as the smoother backup.

Backup option

Raw Suede

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.

Open Bold and smoky

Bold and smoky match

Moon Leather

It gives a strong leather-led evening feel, with Night Smoke ready for anyone wanting the darkest smoky option.

How to sell it

Start with Moon Leather for a bold evening recommendation, then move to Night Smoke if they want something darker and smokier.

Backup option

Night Smoke

Keep this ready if the shopper wants something close, but slightly different.

Go deeper

Jump into the full family section for more cues, talk tracks, and product detail.
